Description: sex, male and female - intersex patients are not considered
chest pain type - typical agina, atypical agina, non-anginal pain, and asymptomatic
resting blood pressure (on admission to the hospital)
whether fasting blood sugar > 120 mg/dl (1 = true; 0 = false)

resting electrocardiographic results. Possible values are normal, having ST-T wave abnormality (T wave inversions and/or ST elevation or depression of > 0.05 mV), or showing probable or definite left ventricular hypertrophy by Estes' criteria. We abbreviate it these values to normal, wave abnormality, left ventricular hypertrophy
ST depression induced by exercise relative to rest
the slope of the peak exercise ST segment. Can be upsloping, flat or downsloping
number of major vessels (0-3) colored by flourosopy
result of thalium stress test. Values can be normal, fixed defect or reversable defect
